page: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 processor, running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture features 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache per core. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 7500 GB local NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 406.47 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 183.77 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% tier. The server also demonstrates top-tier capability in database operations, scoring 47278.09 on Passmark database tests, and multi-core processing with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21629.0. Single-threaded tasks like Gzip compression and Bzip3 decompression register in the top 25% tier, while standard CPU stress tests and encryption benchmarks fall into the middle 50% average.

The server exhibits a clear performance tradeoff when executing large-scale machine learning tasks; prompt processing for a 70B parameter LLM is weak, registering at 5.91 tokens/sec. However, its high local NVMe SSD capacity and top-tier memory bandwidth offer high qualitative cost efficiency for data-dense, non-GPU workloads. This hardware profile is suited for high-throughput database operations, Redis deployments, large-scale software compilation, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. It is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, a 7500 GB local NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results indicate top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds reaching 406.47 GB/sec, and exceptional database performance, scoring 47278.09 in Passmark database operations. While single-threaded compression and multi-core processing are highly competitive, the server exhibits lower performance on large LLM inference tasks. This configuration is engineered for data-dense workloads, including high-throughput databases, Redis caching layers, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server with 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 7500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 7500 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized server featuring dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
256.0 GB of system memory with top-tier large-block read/write bandwidth
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for high-throughput database operations, Redis, and software compilation
